l10n.disableProceedButtonText: Require to complete the current module

params.text:
You are the operations manager of a coffee shop chain.
This week you have a promotion and expect higher traffic.

Your task is to keep service stable, update key numbers clearly, and contact suppliers to resolve a delivery issue.

The first step is choosing the correct workflow from order to customer.

Ready? Let’s go.

alternatives.text: Wait until the courier updates the ETA.
Contact the courier/supplier to confirm what is missing and request release.
Immediately remove all milk drinks from the menu without checking the recovery plan.

branchingQuestion.question: The milk delivery is delayed. The ETA is unknown because a label/document is missing. What should you do first?

params.text:
Waiting is risky. If the ETA is unknown, you may run out of milk.
First confirm what is missing and request release + an updated ETA.

POD is issued after delivery.
Right now you need release confirmation and a new ETA.

That’s a mitigation step, not the first step.
First get a clear recovery plan (release time / updated ETA). Then decide if you need menu changes or a backup order.

Good choice. The ETA is unknown because something is missing.
The fastest way to restore supply is to confirm what is missing (label/document), request release, and get an updated ETA.

Before you act, check the latest KPIs.
This will help you explain the situation clearly.

params.scoreExplanation: Правильные ответы дают +1 балл. Неправильные ответы дают -1 балл. Наименьшая возможная оценка - 0.

params.text: Order
Handover
Quality check
Drink preparation
Payment

params.text:
Now let’s look at today’s constraints.

There are three constraints:

  • One barista called in sick (staff shortage).

  • The espresso machine needs cleaning/maintenance from 14:00–14:30.

  • A delivery of milk is delayed. The courier says the ETA is unknown because a label/document is missing.

Next step: decide what to do first about the milk delay.

params.alt: KPI snapshot (today 12:00 vs yesterday 12:00)  Orders per hour: 42 → 55  Average service time: 3.8 min → 4.6 min  Milk stock (liters): 18 → 6  Waste (milk discarded): 1.2 L → 0.6 L  Customer complaints: 3 → 7

file.alt: KPI dashboard snapshot showing key performance indicators

params.text: Fill in the Blanks
params.notFilledOut: Пожалуйста, заполните все поля для просмотра ответа
params.a11yCheck: Проверьте ответы. Ответы будут помечены как правильные, неправильные или без ответа.
params.a11yShowSolution: Показать ответы. Задание будет отмечена его правильным решением.
params.a11yRetry: Повторить задание. Сбросить все ответы и начать задание заново.
questions:
1) Orders per hour have increased *by* 13. 2) Average service time has increased *to* 4.6 minutes. 3) Milk stock has dropped from *18* to *6* liters. 4) Waste has decreased *by* 0.6 liters.

5) Customer complaints have increased by *4*

choices.question: What is the best short interpretation?
answers:
Demand is hidher, but service time and complaints increased, and milk stock is low. 

Everything improved.

Waste increased, so service improved.

Milk stock is not important.

params.text:
You look in the fridge and realize that milk stock is low. You need to avoid a shortage.

Before moving on to solving the situation, answer a couple of questions 😉

params.text:
Well, the matter turned out to be urgent.
You'd better not delay emailing the supplier

Get going!

alternatives.text: Expedite the current delivery and order from a backup supplier (do both in parallel).

branchingQuestion.question: Alright. You’ve contacted the supplier, but there’s still an urgent risk of running out of milk. What is the best recovery strategy?

params.text:
You need 30 liters of milk urgently.

Compare the offers and choose the best option for urgency.

file.alt: Supplier comparison table: FreshDairy vs QuickMart offers

params.alt: Offer A — FreshDairy: Price €1.10/L | MOQ 30 L | Delivery 18:00 | Delivery included | Net 14 Offer B — QuickMart: Price €1.25/L | MOQ 10 L | Delivery 16:00 | Delivery included | Pay on delivery

file.alt: Supplier comparison details with pricing and delivery terms

params.alt: Offer A — FreshDairy: Price €1.10/L | MOQ 30 L | Delivery 18:00 | Delivery included | Net 14 Offer B — QuickMart: Price €1.25/L | MOQ 10 L | Delivery 16:00 | Delivery included | Pay on delivery

questions:
Issue:

      1.The milk delivery *is delayed* due to a missing label/document. (delay)
      2. The delivery *is held* until the missing information is received. (hold)

Actions:

We *have contacted* the courier/supplier about the milk delay. (contact)

We *have placed* a backup order to protect service. (place)

The supplier *has confirmed* delivery for 16:00. (confirm)

params.text:
Waiting is risky when inventory is low.

It’s 15:30 and there is still no ETA update. Milk stock is very low.
Service time is increasing and complaints are rising.

Good choice. Expediting can restore supply faster if the hold is resolved quickly.
Now set a clear deadline and request an updated ETA.

A backup order reduces risk if the delayed delivery stays on hold.
Now compare offers and choose the best option for urgency.

Excellent! Doing both in parallel reduces risk and protects service during the promotion.
First set deadlines and request release/ETA, then confirm a backup order.

choices.question: ​​​What should you do NOW to keep service stable?
answers:
Limit milk drinks and promote black coffee or drinks with an alternative milk (oat/banana/almond)

Keep selling all milk drinks as usual

Stop serving all drinks

choices.question: Choose the best modal sentence:
answers:
We have to manage demand until milk arrives.

We can manage demand until milk arrives.

We must manage demand until milk arrives.

params.text:
Good news: the courier confirmed the missing label and started processing.
New ETA is 18:00 (later than ideal). Milk is still tight until then.

Waiting did not solve the problem.

You must take action now.

Try to expedite.

metadata.title: Waiting did not solve the problem.

params.text:
Great! The crisis is over.

The courier delivered the backup milk. Now it’s time for the report.

alternatives.text: We are behind schedule and there is no clear plan.
Risk is controlled; we will monitor stock and update you if the ETA changes.
The issue is not urgent, so we will not take further action.
Actions were taken, there is no need to monitor the result.

branchingQuestion.question: Choose the best final line for the report:

metadata.title: Choose the best final line for the report:

params.text:
Thanks!

Good update. The risk is under control and your next steps are clear.

Please create a short memo for the team so everyone follows the same process next time.
Keep it practical: rules for normal days + what to do if a delivery is delayed.

Send it to me when it’s ready.
Look! You've got a message from your boss!
Time to create a memo for a team!

params.notFilledOut: Пожалуйста, заполните все поля для просмотра ответа

questions:
Daily rules

If milk stock *is* low, we *prioritize* drinks with less milk (be, prioritize)

If the queue *is* long, we *assign* one person to support handover (be, assign)

If a drink *is* not OK, we *remake* it before handover (is, remake)

metadata.title: Fill the gaps using Zero or First Conditionals.

params.text: Fill the gaps using Zero or First Conditionals.
params.notFilledOut: Пожалуйста, заполните все поля для просмотра ответа
params.a11yCheck: Проверьте ответы. Ответы будут помечены как правильные, неправильные или без ответа.
params.a11yShowSolution: Показать ответы. Задание будет отмечена его правильным решением.
params.a11yRetry: Повторить задание. Сбросить все ответы и начать задание заново.
questions:
Memo (If problems happen)

 

1) If a key delivery *is* delayed and the ETA is unknown, we *will contact* the courier/supplier and request an updated ETA (be, contact)

 

2) if we *get* no update by 15:00, we *will escalate* the issue to the supervisor (get, escalate)

 

3) If milk *does* not arrive by 16:00, we *will limit* milk-based drinks for one hour and offer alternatives (do, limit)

4) Please follow this process and keep the manager updated if the ETA *changes* (change)

metadata.title: Fill the gaps using Zero or First Conditionals.
